title: Xenial Tunisian Apple Tagine
description: A tangy, snappy tagine that blends the sweetness of apples with a savory bonito-flake twist, perfect for a low-sodium comforting meal.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a heavy skillet over medium heat until shimmering.
Add sliced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic and sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Sprinkle cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and black pepper over the aromatics and cook for 1 minute.
Add diced apples and cook for another 2 minutes to let them soften slightly.
Pour in the applesauce, lemon juice, and lemon zest, stirring to combine.
Sprinkle bonito flakes over the mixture, then season with a pinch of low-sodium salt if desired.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer gently for 20 minutes, or until apples are tender.
Stir occasionally to prevent sticking and adjust seasoning with extra lemon zest if needed.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
